description:
The master_url / federated state model (multiple code repos sharing one balls/tasks hub) was a deliberate scope expansion but shipped by accretion across ~30 commits with no design document. The motivating use case is real: multiple repos syncing to a single upstream did not work under the single-repo orphan-branch model.

The project's three other major subsystems each have a SPEC (forge-gated-delivery, lifecycle-sync-participants, orphan-branch-state) carrying invariants, a backwards-compat audit, and a conformance test list. The federated model is comparably load-bearing -- a nested .balls/state-repo/ clone, symlink-materialized .balls/config.json + .balls/plugins/, the .balls/master.json pointer file, master-wins config policy, and hard-fail-vs-soft-fail reachability semantics -- yet it is documented only as ~71 lines of operational prose in SKILL.md and appears in zero SPECs.

Deliverable: docs/SPEC-federated-state.md, matching the rigor and structure of the existing SPECs. Must cover: the master.json pointer schema, state-repo materialization and re-materializability, the symlink-config seam, master-wins policy, the hard-fail/soft-fail boundary, state_remote (legacy) deprecation, and a backwards/forwards-compatibility section -- the model must stay bilaterally compatible with the simpler standalone model. Include a conformance test list.
title: SPEC the federated multi-repo state model
